name: Somnath Jyotirlinga
description: The first among 12 Jyotirlingas, standing majestically on the Arabian Sea shore. Destroyed and rebuilt 17 times, symbolizing India's resilience.

timings.special: Sound & Light Show at 08:00 PM daily

darshanTypes.name: Aarti Darshan
darshanTypes.description: Special darshan during aarti. Book via somnath.org

darshanTypes.name: VIP Abhishek
darshanTypes.description: Personal abhishek pooja with VIP seating.

importantNotes: Sound & Light Show tickets bookable online
Temple is near the sea — carry shawl for evenings

nearbyPlaces.name: Bhalka Tirth
nearbyPlaces.description: Where Lord Krishna was struck by an arrow

nearbyPlaces.name: Triveni Sangam
nearbyPlaces.description: Confluence of 3 rivers at Arabian Sea

name: Mallikarjuna Swamy
description: Situated atop Nallamala Hills, also a Shakti Peeth. The only temple in India that is both a Jyotirlinga and Shakti Peeth.

darshanTypes.name: Special Darshan
darshanTypes.description: Online timed entry via temple website.

importantNotes: Located in Nallamala forest — scenic drive

nearbyPlaces.name: Srisailam Dam
nearbyPlaces.description: One of India's largest hydroelectric dams on Krishna river

name: Mahakaleshwar Temple
description: The only Dakshinamukhi (south-facing) Jyotirlinga. Located in ancient city of Ujjain on banks of Shipra river. The Bhasma Aarti at dawn is world-famous.

darshanTypes.name: Bhasma Aarti Pass
darshanTypes.description: Witness sacred Bhasma Aarti. Reserve online.

bookingProcess: Select Bhasma Aarti or Special Darshan
importantNotes: Bhasma Aarti requires advance booking — sells out fast
Ujjain is one of 4 Kumbh Mela cities
Visit Ram Ghat for evening aarti

nearbyPlaces.name: Ram Ghat
nearbyPlaces.description: Sacred bathing ghat on river Shipra

nearbyPlaces.name: Kal Bhairav Temple
nearbyPlaces.description: Unique temple where liquor is offered as prasad

nearbyPlaces.name: Vedha Shala (Observatory)
nearbyPlaces.description: Ancient astronomical observatory by Jai Singh

name: Omkareshwar Temple
description: Situated on Mandhata island shaped like the Om symbol in river Narmada. One of 12 Jyotirlingas with breathtaking natural beauty.

timings.special: Parikrama of Om-shaped island takes 2-3 hours

importantNotes: Narmada Parikrama is a sacred walk
Two Jyotirlingas on this island: Omkareshwar and Amareshwar

name: Kedarnath Temple
description: At 11,755 ft in Garhwal Himalayas, a Char Dham and Jyotirlinga. Over 1,000 years old, it survived the 2013 floods miraculously.
bookingProcess: Download certificate and carry with ID

nearbyPlaces.name: Vasuki Tal
nearbyPlaces.description: High altitude lake surrounded by peaks

name: Bhimashankar Temple
description: Nestled in the Sahyadri hills, source of river Bhima. The temple is in a wildlife sanctuary — home to the Indian Giant Squirrel.
importantNotes: Trek route from Khandas village (3 hrs)

name: Kashi Vishwanath Temple
description: One of 12 Jyotirlingas on the western bank of Ganga in Varanasi. The Kashi Vishwanath Corridor has transformed the experience.
importantNotes: Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat — must see

nearbyPlaces.name: BHU Campus
nearbyPlaces.description: Banaras Hindu University with Vishwanath Temple

name: Trimbakeshwar Temple
description: Unique Jyotirlinga with three faces representing Brahma, Vishnu, and Mahesh. Source of Godavari river. One of 4 Kumbh Mela sites.

darshanTypes.name: Kaal Sarp Pooja
darshanTypes.description: Famous Naag pooja — book via trust.

importantNotes: Famous for Kaal Sarp Dosh Pooja

nearbyPlaces.name: Brahmagiri Hill
nearbyPlaces.description: Source of river Godavari — 700 steps trek

nearbyPlaces.name: Anjaneri Fort
nearbyPlaces.description: Birthplace of Lord Hanuman — scenic trek

nearbyPlaces.name: Sula Vineyards
nearbyPlaces.description: India's most famous winery in Nashik

name: Baidyanath Dham
description: Also called Baba Dham, where Ravana worshipped Lord Shiva. The temple complex has 21 other temples within its premises.

timings.special: Shravan Mela draws millions of Kanwariyas

darshanTypes.name: Sparsh Darshan
darshanTypes.description: Touch the Shivalinga. Book via website.

importantNotes: Carry water from Sultanganj (105 km) for Jal Abhishek

nearbyPlaces.name: Nandan Pahar
nearbyPlaces.description: Hill park with cable car rides

nearbyPlaces.name: Tapovan
nearbyPlaces.description: Hot water spring and meditation cave

name: Nageshwar Jyotirlinga
description: Located between Gomti Dwarka and Bait Dwarka island. Famous for the massive 80-ft Shiva statue outside.
bookingProcess: Contact temple office for special pooja
importantNotes: Less crowded compared to other Jyotirlingas

nearbyPlaces.name: Gopi Talav
nearbyPlaces.description: Lake where Gopis are said to have merged

name: Ramanathaswamy Temple
description: A Char Dham site built by Lord Rama. Houses the longest corridor of any Hindu temple. 22 sacred wells for ritual bathing.
importantNotes: Bathe in all 22 Theerthams before darshan
Longest corridor in any Hindu temple

nearbyPlaces.name: Pamban Bridge
nearbyPlaces.description: India's first sea bridge — stunning views

nearbyPlaces.name: Dhanushkodi
nearbyPlaces.description: Ghost town at India's tip — last land before Sri Lanka

nearbyPlaces.name: Adam's Bridge
nearbyPlaces.description: Chain of limestone shoals — Ram Setu

name: Grishneshwar Temple
description: Last of the 12 Jyotirlingas, located near the UNESCO Ellora Caves. A beautiful red stone temple built by Ahilyabai Holkar.

timings.special: Built by Ahilyabai Holkar in 18th century

bookingProcess: Or visit directly — usually less crowded
For special pooja, contact temple office in advance
importantNotes: Near UNESCO Ellora Caves — combine visit

nearbyPlaces.name: Ajanta Caves (UNESCO)
nearbyPlaces.description: 30 Buddhist cave paintings — masterpiece of art

name: Kamakhya Devi Temple
description: Most powerful of the 51 Shakti Peeths atop Nilachal Hill. Known for the Ambubachi Mela when the temple closes for 3 days.
importantNotes: Ambubachi Mela (3 days Jun) — temple closes

nearbyPlaces.name: Umananda Island
nearbyPlaces.description: Smallest river island with Shiva temple

nearbyPlaces.name: Balaji Temple
nearbyPlaces.description: Replica of Tirupati on Nilachal Hill

name: Vaishno Devi Mandir
description: Cave temple at 5,200 ft in Trikuta Mountains. 13 km trek through scenic trails. Yatra registration mandatory.

timings.special: Aarti at 06:00 AM and 07:00 PM daily

bookingProcess: Create account, fill details, select date
Pay and download tickets + Yatra Slip
importantNotes: Without it you CANNOT start trek

nearbyPlaces.name: Patnitop
nearbyPlaces.description: Hill station with snow in winter

name: Tirumala Venkateswara
description: World's most visited and richest temple. 50,000+ daily devotees. Seven Hills of Tirumala. AI-powered crowd management.

darshanTypes.name: Sarva Darshan
darshanTypes.description: Free. Just join queue at Tirumala.

bookingProcess: Pay, download e-ticket, carry with ID
importantNotes: Slots open 2 days in advance at 10 AM

nearbyPlaces.name: Sri Kalahasti Temple
nearbyPlaces.description: Vayu Linga — one of Pancha Bhoota Sthalams

name: Sabarimala Ayyappa
description: Hilltop temple at 1,500 m. Open only during specific seasons. Famous 41-day Mandalam season draws 50 million pilgrims.

timings.special: Open specific seasons only (Nov-Jan, Apr, monthly)

importantNotes: Trek 4.5 km from Pamba to Sannidhanam

nearbyPlaces.name: Pamba River
nearbyPlaces.description: Sacred river — ritual bath before trek

name: Guruvayur Sri Krishna
description: Called the Dwarka of the South. One of the most important Krishna temples. Famous for Tuldabharam (weighing against offerings).
importantNotes: Strict dress code: Men — dhoti, Women — saree/churidar

name: Harmandir Sahib (Golden Temple)
description: Holiest Sikh shrine. Pure gold leaf exterior. Free Langar feeds 100,000+ daily. Open to ALL faiths.

timings.special: Open 20+ hours. Palki Sahib at 10 PM

darshanTypes.name: Free & Open to All
darshanTypes.description: No ticket. No registration. Just visit.

bookingProcess: NO booking needed — temple is FREE and OPEN to all
importantNotes: Head covering mandatory — free at entrance
Best: 4-6 AM (Prakash ceremony) or 10 PM (Palki Sahib)

name: Siddhivinayak Temple
description: Mumbai's most beloved Ganesha temple. 200-year-old right-trunk Ganesh idol considered extremely auspicious.
importantNotes: Tuesdays most crowded — arrive very early

nearbyPlaces.name: Haji Ali Dargah
nearbyPlaces.description: Iconic mosque on islet in the sea

nearbyPlaces.name: Shivaji Park
nearbyPlaces.description: Historic ground — great for evening walks

name: Shirdi Sai Baba Temple
description: One of India's most visited temples. Samadhi Mandir, Dwarkamai, and Chavadi. 25,000+ devotees daily.

timings.special: Kakad Aarti 04:30 AM, Shej Aarti 10:30 PM

importantNotes: Fast Track fills up on weekends
Airport: Shirdi (14 km), Rail: Sainagar Shirdi

nearbyPlaces.name: Dwarkamai
nearbyPlaces.description: Where Sai Baba lived — sacred fire still burns

nearbyPlaces.name: Shani Shingnapur
nearbyPlaces.description: Temple of Lord Shani — village with no doors

name: Ayodhya Ram Mandir
description: Newly built grand Ram temple at Lord Rama's birthplace. Nagara style architecture. India's newest and most anticipated temple.

darshanTypes.name: Advance Slot Booking
darshanTypes.description: Free but advance slot booking required online.

nearbyPlaces.name: Kanak Bhawan
nearbyPlaces.description: Temple of Sita-Ram with gold ornaments

nearbyPlaces.name: Saryu River Ghat
nearbyPlaces.description: Sacred bathing ghats on river Saryu

name: Badrinath Temple
description: At 10,279 ft between Nar and Narayan ranges. Char Dham and Divya Desam. Stunning Himalayan backdrop.

timings.special: Open Apr-Nov. Maha Abhishek at 04:30 AM

importantNotes: Road can be blocked by landslides

nearbyPlaces.name: Mana Village
nearbyPlaces.description: Last Indian village before Tibet border

name: Jagannath Temple Puri
description: Char Dham site. 12th-century temple. World's largest kitchen — feeds 100,000 daily. Famous Rath Yatra.
importantNotes: Non-Hindus: View from Raghunandan Library rooftop

name: Meenakshi Amman Temple
description: 2,500-year-old Dravidian masterpiece. 14 gopurams with thousands of colorful sculptures. Hall of 1000 Pillars.
importantNotes: Photography allowed in outer corridors only

nearbyPlaces.name: Alagar Koil
nearbyPlaces.description: Hill temple of Lord Vishnu in scenic setting

name: Dwarkadhish Temple
description: Char Dham site — Krishna's legendary kingdom. 5-storey temple with 72 pillars. Submerged city of Dwarka nearby.
bookingProcess: For special pooja, contact temple office
importantNotes: Bait Dwarka island temple — ferry ride

nearbyPlaces.name: Bait Dwarka
nearbyPlaces.description: Island temple — ferry ride from Okha

name: Padmanabhaswamy Temple
description: 108 Divya Desams. $20 billion+ treasure in vaults. Strict Dravidian traditions. Royal Travancore patronage.
bookingProcess: Pay online and carry receipt + ID

nearbyPlaces.name: Napier Museum
nearbyPlaces.description: Gothic style museum with art collection

name: Khatu Shyam Ji Temple
description: Temple of Barbarik (grandson of Bhima), known as Khatu Shyam. One of Rajasthan's most visited temples.
bookingProcess: Direct visit — no online booking required
Special poojas can be arranged at temple

name: Mahabodhi Temple
description: UNESCO World Heritage. Where Buddha attained enlightenment under the Bodhi Tree. Most sacred Buddhist site in the world.
importantNotes: The Bodhi Tree is a descendant of the original
Open to ALL — no religious restriction

nearbyPlaces.name: Bodhi Tree
nearbyPlaces.description: Descendant of tree where Buddha attained enlightenment

nearbyPlaces.name: Sujata Stupa
nearbyPlaces.description: Where Sujata offered kheer to Buddha

name: Amarnath Cave Temple
description: Sacred ice Shivalinga forms naturally in a cave at 12,756 ft. Annual yatra (Jun-Aug) draws 3 lakh+ pilgrims.

timings.special: Open only Jun-Aug. Ice lingam forms naturally

bookingProcess: Get medical fitness certificate from registered hospital
Report at Pahalgam or Baltal base camp
importantNotes: Trek: 46 km from Pahalgam / 14 km from Baltal

nearbyPlaces.name: Pahalgam
nearbyPlaces.description: Valley of Shepherds — trek starting point

nearbyPlaces.name: Betaab Valley
nearbyPlaces.description: Stunning valley named after Bollywood film

name: Srirangam Ranganathaswamy
description: Largest functioning Hindu temple in the world. 156 acres with 21 gopurams and 7 enclosures. First among 108 Divya Desams.
importantNotes: Non-Hindus allowed in outer enclosures only

nearbyPlaces.name: Rock Fort Temple
nearbyPlaces.description: Temple atop massive rock in Trichy

name: Arunachaleswarar Temple
description: Fire element among 5 Pancha Bhoota Sthalams. Karthigai Deepam festival lights a massive fire visible for miles. Ramana Maharshi's ashram nearby.
importantNotes: Girivalam (14 km walk) on full moon is a must
Karthigai Deepam (Nov-Dec) — massive flame on hill

name: Palani Murugan Temple
description: One of the six abodes of Lord Murugan. Hilltop temple with winch and ropeway. Panchamirtham prasadam is world-famous.

darshanTypes.name: General Darshan
darshanTypes.description: Free. Trek or ropeway to hilltop.

nearbyPlaces.name: Palani Hills
nearbyPlaces.description: Scenic hill range — part of Western Ghats

name: Salasar Balaji Temple
description: Famous self-manifested (Swayambhu) idol of Lord Hanuman. One of the most visited Hanuman temples in India.

name: Lingaraj Temple
description: 11th-century Kalinga architecture masterpiece. 180-ft tall deul (tower). Largest temple in Bhubaneswar — temple city of India.

timings.special: Non-Hindus view from a raised platform outside

name: Banke Bihari Temple
description: One of the most revered Krishna temples in Vrindavan. Unique tradition — curtain conceals deity, revealed periodically.
bookingProcess: Arrive early to avoid extreme crowds
importantNotes: Extremely crowded during Holi and Janmashtami

nearbyPlaces.name: Prem Mandir
nearbyPlaces.description: Stunning white marble Krishna temple — lit at night

nearbyPlaces.name: Krishna Janmabhoomi
nearbyPlaces.description: Birthplace of Lord Krishna in Mathura

name: Konark Sun Temple
description: UNESCO World Heritage. 13th-century chariot-shaped temple. 24 carved wheels and 7 horses. Masterpiece of Kalinga architecture.
bookingProcess: Visit konark.nic.in or buy ticket at gate
ASI monument — ticket counter at entrance
importantNotes: Best at sunrise — temple faces east

nearbyPlaces.name: Chandrabhaga Beach
nearbyPlaces.description: Sacred beach — first rays of sun on chariot temple

name: Akshardham Temple
description: Guinness World Record for largest Hindu temple. Stunning Rajasthani-pink sandstone and Italian marble. Musical fountain show.

timings.special: Water show at 07:15 PM (seasonal). Closed Mondays.

bookingProcess: No advance booking — buy ticket at gate

nearbyPlaces.name: ISKON Delhi
nearbyPlaces.description: Hare Krishna temple — Glory of India exhibition

name: Yadagirigutta Temple
description: Newly renovated grand temple of Lord Narasimha on Yadagiri hills. Modeled after Tirumala — fast becoming South India's next mega-pilgrimage.
bookingProcess: Pay online and download QR pass

nearbyPlaces.name: Hyderabad
nearbyPlaces.description: City of Charminar and Golconda Fort

name: Vitthal Rukmini Temple
description: Central shrine of the Varkari tradition. Millions of devotees walk (Wari/Dindi) to Pandharpur on Ashadhi and Kartiki Ekadashi.
